Class Target

java.lang.Object
component.Target
public final class Target extends Object
Represents a target container used for skill execution.

A Target instance can represent either:

  • a single Unit
  • a group of Unit objects

This abstraction allows skills to accept a unified parameter while supporting both single-target and multi-target abilities.

Exactly one of single() or many() will be non-null.

    Single target unit (used for single-target skills).

  • Field Details

    • single

      private final Unit single
      Single target unit (used for single-target skills).

    • group

      private final List<? extends Unit> group
      Group of target units (used for multi-target skills).

  • Constructor Details

    • Target

      private Target(Unit single, List<? extends Unit> group)
      Private constructor. Use factory methods one(Unit) or many(List).
      Parameters:
      single - single unit target (or null)
      group - list of target units (or null)

  • Method Details

    • one

      public static Target one(Unit u)
      Creates a Target representing a single unit.
      Parameters:
      u - the target unit
      Returns:
      a Target in single-target mode

    • many

      public static Target many(List<? extends Unit> units)
      Creates a Target representing multiple units.
      Parameters:
      units - list of target units
      Returns:
      a Target in multi-target mode

    • single

      public Unit single()
      Returns the single target.
      Returns:
      single unit, or null if this is a multi-target

    • many

      public List<? extends Unit> many()
      Returns the list of targets.
      Returns:
      list of units, or null if this is a single-target

    • isSingle

      public boolean isSingle()
      Checks whether this Target represents a single unit.
      Returns:
      true if single-target mode

    • isMany

      public boolean isMany()
      Checks whether this Target represents multiple units.
      Returns:
      true if multi-target mode
